testcases.input: arr = [0,10,20,30], fn = function greaterThan10(n) { return n > 10; } Output: [20,30] Explanation: const newArray = filter(arr, fn); // [20, 30] The function filters out values that are not greater than 10
testcases.expectedOutput: [20,30] Explanation: const newArray = filter(arr, fn); // [20, 30] The function filters out values that are not greater than 10

testcases.input: arr = [1,2,3], fn = function firstIndex(n, i) { return i === 0; } Output: [1] Explanation: fn can also accept the index of each element In this case, the function removes elements not at index 0
testcases.expectedOutput: [1] Explanation: fn can also accept the index of each element In this case, the function removes elements not at index 0

testcases.input: arr = [-2,-1,0,1,2], fn = function plusOne(n) { return n + 1 } Output: [-2,0,1,2] Explanation: Falsey values such as 0 should be filtered out
testcases.expectedOutput: [-2,0,1,2] Explanation: Falsey values such as 0 should be filtered out
